name: Mung Bean Pancakes- Bindae Duk
recipeInstructions:
1. Mung beans have been peeled and split in half.
Substitute soybeans, canned chickpeas (garbanzos), or yellow lentils.
2. Soak the mung beans overnight in cold water, then drain well.
3. Place the mung beans in a food processor, blender, or mortar and grind to a fairly smooth paste, adding about 1 cup of cold water.
Stir in the remaining ingredients (except the oils), beating until well mixed.
If you don't have kim chee, use finely chopped Chinese cabbage and add a little chili powder to the pancake batter.
4. Heat a heavy skillet and oil lightly with a mixture of vegetable and sesame oils.
5. Drop spoonfuls of the batter onto the skillet and cook until golden underneath, with small bubbles appearing on the surface.
6. Flip over and cook the other side.
7. As they cook, stack the pancakes under a cloth until the remainder are done.
Serve hot or cold with chili and soy sauce dips.
